product.name: ArShirt Moda Pro
product.familyDescriptorPl: Robocze modowe — stretch i polar, wielobrandowe
product.familyDescriptorEn: Fashion workwear — stretch and fleece, multi-brand
product.categorySourceGroup: High Visible Warp Knit Fabrics for Safety Jackets
product.titleDescriptor: 60% Modacrylic 38% Cotton 2% Carbon, Inherent Pique Flame Retardant Antistatic Fabric
product.specLine: 220 g/m², 60% Modacrylic 38% Cotton 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant
descriptions: Antistatic Pique Knit Fabric 60% modacrylic 38% cotton 2% carbon, inherent flame retardant antistatic knit fabric . ArShirt Moda Pro fabric are mostly used by welders, electrical workers and firefighters.
It is resistant to very high sun level, with fastness level of 6 to 7.
ArShirt Moda Pro are inherent flame retardant with EN 11612 certification.
Suitable for antistatic applications, certified for EN ISO 1149-5
It provides arc flash protection with EN 61482 class 1 (4 kA) certification.
60% Modacrylic 38% Cotton 2% Carbon, Inherent Pique Flame Retardant Antistatic Fabric
220 g/m², 60% Modacrylic 38% Cotton 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant
technicalParameters: Dimensional Change | -5% < Length < +5% ; -5% < Width < +5% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Done | EN ISO 11612

acceptedMatches.rationale: Explicit hi-vis category and EN ISO 20471 certification directly support high-visibility apparel classification.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Description explicitly names electrical workers as intended users, combined with arc-flash and antistatic certifications directly tied to electrical hazard protection.
Description explicitly names firefighters as intended users, though this is turnout-gear-adjacent workwear rather than explicit structural firefighting gear.
No explicit military, police or tactical use stated in the product data.
Description explicitly states welders as primary users, supported by arc-flash protection certification relevant to welding hazards.

evidence: categorySourceGroup: High Visible Warp Knit Fabrics for Safety Jackets
primary.rationale: The product is explicitly categorized as a hi-vis warp knit fabric for safety jackets and carries EN ISO 20471 certification, making high-visibility apparel the clearest and most direct commercial use.

evidence: Description: 'mostly used by welders, electrical workers and firefighters'
secondary.rationale: Arc-flash protection (EN 61482) and antistatic certification (EN 1149) directly correspond to electrical hazard protection for electrical workers, an explicitly named user group, supporting energy sector as an independent secondary use.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Welders are named as users but no molten-metal splash or foundry-specific evidence is given beyond generic FR/arc protection shared with electrical work; energy sector evidence is more specific via EN 61482 arc-flash class.
Firefighters are mentioned as users but the product lacks EN 469 turnout gear certification or structural firefighting framing typical of dedicated firefighting fabrics.
No military, police or tactical use stated.
A more specific named sector (hivis) dominates the product positioning.

modelOutput.reviewReason: Description names three distinct hazard-based user groups (welders, electrical workers, firefighters) with overlapping FR/arc/antistatic certifications, making the single secondary application choice somewhat ambiguous.
